balance_summary:
NEW HEROES

Hero Dark Prince

Ability: Destructive Dismount

What’s better than a charging horse? A charging Rhino!

Like his rider, Rhino has endured many a battle. Now that he and Dark Prince have found each other, they can break through any tower, or Skelly, together.

Fun fact: Dark Prince tried to lead the charge on his own. It was a bold move with an unfortunate outcome…


Hero Bowler

Ability: Stone Swish

From clean strikes to flawless three-pointers, a true legend of the Arena.

After baseball broke the bleachers and football flattened all the Goblins, they found their way onto the court. With his ability, Hero Bowler plants his feet and sinks three-pointers across the Arena. Keep his range in mind and make every shot count!

Balance Update – May 4th
“Bring balance to the Arena, don’t leave it in darkness!” - Blue King

NERFS ⬇️

Hero Barbarian Barrel

Reroll Range: 4 tiles → 3 tiles (-25%)

Hero Barbarian Barrel has been too focused on rolling to Crown Towers for chip damage. This change shifts power back toward its defensive role, making it more reliable for clearing troops but less likely to reach towers.


Hero Mega Minion

Warp Damage: 412 → 373 (-9%)

Hero Mega Minion now takes an extra swipe to finish off some mid-hitpoint troops, reducing how often he turns his attention to Crown Towers.


Goblin Drill Evolution

Goblins Spawned on Second Submerge: 2 → 1 (-50%)

The latest buff to Evolved Goblin Drill made it pop up right at the top of the Ranked meta. We're reverting this change and will monitor its performance before deciding on an alternate balance direction.


Royal Hogs Evolution

Landing Damage: 84 → 43 (-48%)

These guys really knew how to pork a punch! Going airborne proved too valuable for this win condition, so it’ll now take the landing of two Evolved Royal Hogs to take out Skeletons.


Giant Snowball Evolution

Slowdown Duration: 4s → 3s (-25%)

Evolved Giant Snowball is too effective at defensive stalling, so we're standardizing the slow duration with its unevolved form.


Tesla Evolution

Pulse Damage: 174 → 148 (-15%)

Evolved Tesla provides too much utility with its high area damage and stun. We’re shifting power out of the damage, so that this Evolution is better defined by its stun mechanic.


Dark Prince

Hit Speed: 1.3s → 1.4s (+8%)

Dark Prince charged into the meta after his buff last year. We're toning down his hit speed to make him less intimidating on offense.


Skeleton Dragons

Damage: 161 → 151 (-6%)

After increasing their splash radius to match other AoE options, swarm troops have definitely had a bone to pick with the Skeleton Dragons. A damage nerf will keep their versatility in check.


Spirit Empress (Ground)

Hit Speed: 1.1s → 1.2s (+9%)

Hitpoints: 1152 → 1121 (-3%)


Spirit Empress (Air)

Hitpoints: 1177 → 1121 (-5%)

Spirit Empress has been riding a bit too high across the skies and the turf. We’re trimming down her hit speed and hitpoints without shaking up key interactions.

BUFFS ⬆️

Electro Giant

Hit Speed: 2.1s → 1.8 (-14%)

First Hit Speed: 1.5s → 1s (-33%)

Reflected Tower Damage: 50 → 38 (-24%)



Electro Giant is shockingly effective when he gets to a Crown Tower, but buildings have been cutting his power. This rework keeps his damage output against Crown Towers relatively the same while helping him break through buildings.

REWORKS 🔄

Giant Skeleton

Hitpoints: 1413 → 1313 (-7%)

Collision Radius: 1 tile → 0.75 tiles (-25%)

Death Bomb Damage: 209 → 269 (+29%)

Death Bomb Tower Damage: 418 → 269 (-36%)

Giant Skeleton has historically been inconsistent, since the Death Bomb rarely decides battles unless it reaches a Crown Tower. We’re adjusting his stats so the bomb pays off defensively more often, while preserving most of its Crown Tower threat.

Remember! Cards are scaled at Level 11 and, most of the time, base form changes also affect a card’s Evolution and Hero forms.

archetypes.name: Cycle
archetypes.description: Cycle decks keep average elixir low, defend with cheap reusable answers, and win by reaching the same win condition or spell sequence faster than the opponent can reset. Hog, Miner, Royal Hogs, X-Bow, and fast Drill lists can all belong here when speed and rotation are the main identity.

archetypes.name: Control
archetypes.description: Control decks absorb pressure efficiently, trade up on defense, and win through a measured counter-push or repeated safe damage. Graveyard control, Drill control, Royal Giant control, and defensive Hog or Nado styles belong here when the core identity is reaction-first play and advantage conversion.

archetypes.name: Beatdown
archetypes.description: Beatdown decks invest in a tank or heavy front line, accept some early damage, and try to win by building one push that becomes too expensive to stop cleanly. Giant, Golem, Electro Giant, Goblin Giant, and similar ground tanks belong here when the main plan is stacking support behind a front line.

archetypes.name: Siege
archetypes.description: Siege decks deal tower damage primarily through long-range buildings such as X-Bow or Mortar, then protect that building or reuse it as a defensive anchor. Some lists play very fast cycle while others lean more control or bait, but the siege building must still be the main structural win condition.

archetypes.name: Bait
archetypes.description: Bait decks overload the opponent's small spells or splash answers by presenting multiple punish cards that demand the same counter window. Goblin Barrel, Princess, Skeleton Barrel, Dart Goblin, Gang, and related pressure cards are common, but the core identity is counter exhaustion rather than any one exact card.

archetypes.description: Bridge Spam decks win by turning small openings into immediate pressure at the river. Battle Ram, Bandit, Royal Ghost, Ram Rider, Magic Archer, and PEKKA support pieces are typical, but the defining trait is repeated fast bridge pressure that denies the opponent time to set up.

archetypes.name: Split-Lane Pressure
archetypes.description: Split-Lane Pressure decks win by forcing the opponent to answer threats in both lanes instead of building one clean defense. Royal Hogs, Wall Breakers, Giant Skeleton pressure cores, and fast bridge threats often appear here, but the defining trait is multi-lane strain rather than pure bridge spam or pure bait.

archetypes.name: Spawner
archetypes.description: Spawner decks use Goblin Hut, Furnace, Barbarian Hut, or repeated building-based token pressure to tax splash, spells, and lane management over time. Their identity is attrition and nuisance pressure that keeps the board occupied, not one single all-in push.

archetypes.name: Air Beatdown
archetypes.description: Air Beatdown decks build heavy flying pushes that are difficult to answer once the tank and support troops cross the river. They usually spend single elixir defending and setting up, then try to overwhelm the opponent with a layered Lava Hound or Balloon assault in double elixir.

archetypes.name: Three Musketeers / Pump
archetypes.description: Three Musketeers / Pump decks revolve around Elixir Collector and split-lane macro pressure. They try to create an elixir lead, then divide Three Musketeers across lanes or pair them with a tank to force the opponent's big spell and defensive answers into impossible trade-offs.

archetypes.name: Mortar Bait
archetypes.description: Mortar Bait is the siege-bait subtype where Mortar and a secondary nuisance threat such as Skeleton Barrel share the pressure load while Cannon Cart, Goblin Gang, Rascals, or similar sticky support pieces make clean answers awkward. The deck still hinges on Mortar plus layered bait pressure rather than on pure cycle siege or plain Miner control.

archetypes.name: Giant Graveyard
archetypes.description: Giant Graveyard is the Giant-centered Graveyard subtype where Giant tanks for Graveyard while Bowler, Dark Prince, Witch, Archer Queen, and similar medium-cost support cards turn defense into one heavy counter-push. The deck is defined by Giant plus Graveyard as the payoff pair, not by pure Splashyard-style spell cycling or by plain Giant beatdown without a Graveyard finish.

archetypes.name: Spawner Bait
archetypes.description: Spawner Bait is the overlap subtype between hut-based attrition and bait, where Goblin Hut, Furnace, or another repeat-token building taxes small spells and lane management before a separate punish card converts that distraction into real tower damage. The defining trait is that both the spawner engine and the bait finisher matter; neither one alone fully explains the deck.
archetypes.notes: Thin overlap subtype by design. Use it only when a real spawner engine and a real bait finisher are both central to how the deck wins. Keep `popular_decks` empty unless repeated clean examples appear; if either side of the overlap is only incidental, fall back to Spawner, Bait, or another more specific subtype.

archetypes.name: Royal Hogs Goblin Hut Lightning
archetypes.description: Royal Hogs Goblin Hut Lightning is a spawner-pressure shell where Goblin Hut keeps one lane occupied while Royal Hogs force split-lane answers and Lightning punishes stacked defensive answers. Royal Ghost, Wizard, Electro Spirit, and a cheap support slot round out the list, but the defining identity is pigs plus hut attrition backed by Lightning rather than Mega Knight spawner control or pure Royal Hogs cycle.

archetypes.notes: Use this when Goblin Hut is still a central pressure engine and Royal Hogs plus Lightning provide the main punish plan. If the hut is only incidental, prefer a broader Royal Hogs pressure subtype; if Mega Knight or a double-spawner control core becomes the real center, prefer the other spawner shell instead.

archetypes.name: GS 3M Heal Control
archetypes.description: GS 3M Heal Control is a Three Musketeers / Pump subtype built around Elixir Collector, Giant Skeleton, and split-lane pressure. Giant Skeleton buys time on defense and clogs the bridge, then the deck converts that space into a divided Three Musketeers push instead of one classic beatdown stack.

archetypes.notes: Narrow Three Musketeers subtype. Use this when Elixir Collector, Giant Skeleton, and split-lane 3M conversion are the defining macro plan. Do not use it as a default label for every 3M or Giant Skeleton deck; if Giant tanking or another 3M structure explains the list better, prefer that other subtype.

archetypes.name: Goblin Giant Sparky
archetypes.description: Goblin Giant Sparky is the Sparky beatdown subtype where Goblin Giant tanks for Sparky while Dark Prince, Electro Wizard, and a flex heavy-damage support slot stabilize defense and threaten the counter-push. Rage or Heal Spirit variants exist, but the defining identity is still Goblin Giant plus Sparky forcing one explosive ground push through the river.

archetypes.name: Giant Skeleton Royal Hogs
archetypes.description: Giant Skeleton Royal Hogs is the heavy Royal Hogs subtype that pairs Royal Hogs with a defensive core of Giant Skeleton, Fisherman, Zappies, and Mother Witch. Royal Ghost, Mirror, or Skeleton Army variants can rotate around that core, but the defining plan is still sturdy defense into awkward two-lane Royal Hogs pressure.

archetypes.notes: Recognizable heavy Royal Hogs subtype. Use this when the Giant Skeleton plus Fisherman and Zappies backbone is what makes the pigs pressure work. If that heavy defensive core is missing, prefer a broader Royal Hogs pressure label or another split-lane subtype.

archetypes.name: GobHut Bait / Rascals Bait Control
archetypes.description: GobHut Bait / Rascals Bait Control is a heavier bait-control subtype that uses Goblin Hut pressure and a bulky defensive core to overload the opponent's answers over time. Unlike fast classic Log Bait, it wins through attrition, repeated nuisance pressure, and delayed punish cards such as Goblin Barrel, Mirror, or swarm follow-up rather than pure low-cost cycling.

archetypes.notes: Heavy bait-control subtype. Use this for Goblin Hut lists that win by slow answer exhaustion, nuisance pressure, and delayed bait punish cards rather than by fast classic Log Bait sequencing. If Goblin Hut is only incidental, fall back to Spawner; if the deck is much faster and more spell-exhaustion focused, prefer a cleaner Bait subtype.

archetypes.name: Giant Beatdown
archetypes.description: Giant Beatdown is the simpler Giant subtype where Giant fronts a compact support package such as Witch, Dark Prince, Mini P.E.K.K.A, Bats, and Rage instead of leaning on Graveyard, Sparky, or Pump macro play. The deck usually wins by defending efficiently, then dropping Giant in front of surviving support for one direct bridge-crossing push.

archetypes.name: Elixir Golem Beatdown
archetypes.description: Elixir Golem Beatdown is the all-in beatdown subtype where Elixir Golem is the central build-around card and the deck accepts the elixir split risk in exchange for one overwhelming double-elixir push. Battle Healer, Electro Dragon, Baby Dragon, Tornado, and similar sustain or splash packages are the usual support frame, and the subtype is distinct from broader Giant or Golem beatdown because the Elixir Golem payoff and risk profile define the deck.

archetypes.name: Giant Sparky Beatdown
archetypes.description: Giant Sparky Beatdown is the Giant-frontline Sparky subtype where Giant absorbs the bridge damage tax, Sparky provides the burst threat, and Wizard, Mega Minion, Bats, Skeleton Army, or other mid-cost support pieces stabilize defense before the main push forms. It is distinct from Goblin Giant Sparky because the tank and support geometry are different even though both are Sparky beatdown patterns.

archetypes.name: Mega Hogs / Royal Hogs Fireball Bait
archetypes.description: Mega Hogs / Royal Hogs Fireball Bait is a split-lane subtype where Royal Hogs supply the main punish card while Mega Knight, Zappies, Inferno Dragon, and cheap support troops create awkward defensive sequencing on the return push. The defining identity is defensive Mega Knight into Royal Hogs counter-pressure, not simple pigs cycle and not pure Mega Knight bridge spam.

archetypes.name: Mega Knight Wall Breakers
archetypes.description: Mega Knight Wall Breakers is the split-lane subtype where Mega Knight handles the heaviest defensive trades, then Wall Breakers and a second pressure card such as Miner, Bandit, Archer Queen, or Prince force the opponent to defend both lanes at once. The subtype is defined by Mega Knight plus Wall Breakers pressure conversion, not by pure Miner cycle and not by heavier bridge-spam stacks without the split-lane Wall Breakers core.

archetypes.name: Goblin Drill Control
archetypes.description: Goblin Drill Control is the heavier Drill subtype where Goblin Drill supplies repeatable chip while a sturdy defensive package such as Knight, Barbarians, Cannon Cart, Electro Wizard, Mother Witch, and sometimes Goblin Demolisher wins trades first. Unlike faster Drill cycle or bait lists, this subtype leans on thick defense and counter-push conversion rather than on nonstop cheap pressure.

archetypes.name: Goblin Hut/Spawner Control
archetypes.description: Goblin Hut/Spawner Control is the heavier hut-control subtype where Goblin Hut or a double-spawner core occupies the board over time while a sturdier defensive backbone and one heavier punish card convert that chip into safe tower damage. It sits between pure attrition Spawner decks and more explicit bait patterns, and it should only be used when the hut engine still drives the game plan.

If the bait finisher becomes more important than the hut engine, prefer Spawner Bait; if Graveyard or another named win condition clearly defines the deck, prefer that more specific subtype.

archetypes.name: LavaLoon
archetypes.description: LavaLoon is the Lava Hound plus Balloon subtype where Lava Hound absorbs anti-air attention so Balloon or a follow-up spell can break through behind it. Mighty Miner, Void, Clone, Skeleton Dragons, Tombstone, and hero support can rotate around that core, but the subtype is defined by Lava Hound plus Balloon as the main payoff pair rather than by generic air stacking.

archetypes.name: Mega Knight Bait / Double Spawner Control
archetypes.description: Mega Knight Bait / Double Spawner Control is a heavy spawner-control subtype where Furnace plus Goblin Hut keep both lanes occupied while Mega Knight absorbs defensive burden and punishes overcommitment. Archer Queen, Minions, and Goblin Gang are common support pieces, but the defining identity is repeated board clutter plus defensive Mega Knight conversion rather than simple Mega Knight bridge pressure.

archetypes.name: 3M Giant Pump
archetypes.description: 3M Giant Pump is the classic Three Musketeers / Pump subtype where Elixir Collector and Giant create the payoff line instead of Giant Skeleton control or faster bridge-pressure variants. Prince, Minions, Skeleton Army, and small-spell support can rotate around that core, but the subtype is still defined by Giant tanking for split Three Musketeers after a Pump-led elixir setup.

archetypes.name: Golem Beatdown
archetypes.description: Golem Beatdown is the heavy Golem subtype where Golem is the unquestioned front line and the rest of the deck is built to escort one expensive push across the river. Elixir Collector, Prince, Wizard, Cannon Cart, Lumberjack, Electro Dragon, and Baby Dragon are all common support choices, but the subtype is defined by committing to Golem and amplifying one slow push rather than by split pressure or fast cycling.

archetypes.name: Giant Skeleton Clone
archetypes.description: Giant Skeleton Clone is the cheese Giant Skeleton subtype where Giant Skeleton pressure is built specifically to make Clone lethal, usually with Skeleton Barrel, Skeleton King, Bats, Flying Machine, or swarm support creating a cloned death-bomb or cloned swarm finish. archetypes.name: Miner Balloon Cycle
archetypes.description: Miner Balloon Cycle is the fast Miner plus Balloon subtype where Miner tanks for Balloon while the rest of the deck stays light enough to keep that pairing rotating. Ice Golem, Guards, Inferno Dragon, Ice Spirit, Poison, Zap, and other cheap defensive cards can fill the support slots, but the defining identity is still Miner plus Balloon in a low-cost control frame rather than a heavier air beatdown deck.

If Lava Hound, Lumberjack, or a heavier Balloon support stack becomes the real center of the deck, prefer Air Beatdown, LumberLoon Freeze, or another more specific Balloon subtype.

Cannon, Musketeer, Ice Golem, Skeletons, Ice Spirit, and The Log are the defining support core, even when evolutions or hero variants replace the plain versions.

archetypes.description: PEKKA Bridge Spam is the classic PEKKA river-pressure shell where Battle Ram, Bandit, Royal Ghost, and cheap spell support convert defensive stops into immediate bridge punishment. Electro Wizard and Magic Archer are the most common support pair, while Fireball and Poison variants rotate around the same pressure core.

archetypes.name: Electro Giant Lightning Tornado

Cannon, Bomber, Phoenix, Golden Knight, Little Prince, Bowler, and Barbarian Barrel can rotate through the support slots, but the subtype is defined by clearing the path for Electro Giant with Lightning plus Tornado rather than by slow Golem-style stacking.

archetypes.name: X-Bow Cycle
archetypes.description: X-Bow Cycle is the classic fast siege shell where X-Bow is protected by Tesla and a very cheap defensive core so the deck can rotate back to its win condition repeatedly. Archers, Knight, Skeletons, Fireball, The Log, and a one-cost spirit keep the structure stable even when current evolutions or hero variants replace the plain versions.

archetypes.name: Goblin Drill Magic Archer Control
archetypes.description: Goblin Drill Magic Archer Control is the current Drill control shell where Goblin Drill supplies repeatable chip while Magic Archer, Bomb Tower, Tornado, and cheap ground support create tight defensive conversions. Valkyrie, Skeletons, Spear Goblins, and The Log are the most common glue cards, but the subtype is defined by Drill pressure plus Magic Archer control angles rather than by heavier counter-push bodies.

archetypes.name: Archer Queen Royal Hogs Cycle
archetypes.description: Archer Queen Royal Hogs Cycle is the fast Royal Hogs shell where Archer Queen, Cannon, Earthquake, Royal Delivery, and cheap cycle support keep the deck light enough to pressure repeatedly without relying on heavier split-lane anchors. Skeletons, Fire Spirit, and The Log are common glue cards, but the identity is specifically Queen-backed pigs cycle rather than Recruits pressure or Giant Skeleton support.

archetypes.name: Hog EQ Mighty Miner
archetypes.description: Hog EQ Mighty Miner is the modern Hog cycle shell where Hog Rider plus Earthquake remain the win-condition pair while Mighty Miner and Firecracker add more staying power than the older 2.6-style builds. Bomb Tower or Tesla, Goblins or Skeletons, Ice Spirit or Electro Spirit, and The Log or Barbarian Barrel are the most common support rotations, but the subtype is defined by that Hog plus Earthquake plus Mighty Miner structure rather than by Cannon and Musketeer classic cycle.

archetypes.name: Bandit Cart Log Bait
archetypes.description: Bandit Cart Log Bait is the heavier modern bait shell where Goblin Barrel and Princess still exhaust small-spell answers, but Bandit, Cannon Cart, Dark Prince, and Rascals make the deck more punishing and board-sticky than classic fast bait. The identity is still bait pressure first, just with bulkier bridge and support bodies than the classic Inferno Tower version.

archetypes.name: Lava Miner
archetypes.description: Lava Miner is the Lava Hound subtype where Miner supplies the secondary win-condition pressure instead of Balloon, letting the deck convert Lava Hound tanking into repeated Miner chip and awkward split damage. Skeleton Dragons, Mega Minion, Flying Machine, Tombstone, Arrows, and heavier support variants such as Phoenix, Barbarians, Poison, or Lightning can rotate around that core, but the defining payoff pair is still Lava Hound plus Miner rather than generic air stacking.

If Balloon becomes the explicit second payoff, prefer LavaLoon; if Lava Hound disappears, move to another Miner or Balloon shell instead of using Air Beatdown as a catch-all.

archetypes.name: Lumberjack Giant Skeleton Spam
archetypes.description: Bridge spam shell built around the Giant Skeleton as a bridge pusher and tank. Lumberjack leads the push, leaving a rage spell on death that lets the Giant Skeleton detonate earlier and deal massive damage. Support from Zappies or Electro Spirit handles air threats and cycles quickly.

archetypes.name: Ram Rider Bridge Spam
archetypes.description: Bridge spam shell centred on Ram Rider as the primary win condition. Lumberjack provides a rage buff on death that amplifies Ram Rider's charge damage significantly. Furnace chip damage and a spawner core apply constant pressure while the Ram Rider threatens the tower on every counter-push.

archetypes.name: Mega Knight Bridge Spam
archetypes.description: Bridge spam shell using Mega Knight as the heavy anchor that resets the board on a jump-spawn or counter-push. Bandit and Lumberjack add fast bridge threats. Night Witch or Wall Breakers provide secondary pressure. A reliable Fireball cycle clears value troops and secures three-crown finishes.

archetypes.description: Fast siege-cycle shell that pairs Mortar chip damage with Miner as a secondary win condition and tower sniper. Poison protects both the Mortar and the Miner push. Bats and Ice Spirit cycle quickly back to the Mortar for relentless pressure. Very hard to out-cycle in the late game.

archetypes.name: Mortar Cannon Cart Bait
archetypes.description: Hybrid siege-bait shell combining Mortar chip pressure with Cannon Cart as a secondary charging tank and bait-winning threat. Goblin barrel, Goblin Gang, and Princess force The Log and Fireball responses so the Mortar and Cannon Cart can connect unchallenged. One of the most-played competitive siege builds.

archetypes.name: Goblin Barrel Firecracker Cycle
archetypes.description: Fast bait-cycle shell that replaces the usual Princess with Firecracker as the ranged bait unit. Firecracker chips from a safer distance and is harder to The Log away than Princess. Goblin Barrel remains the primary direct-damage threat. Very low elixir average enables rapid recycling through all bait cards.

archetypes.name: Lava Hound Skeleton Dragons
archetypes.description: Air beatdown shell pairing the classic Lava Hound frontline with Skeleton Dragons as the primary DPS air unit behind it. Skeleton Dragons blanket wide areas on both offense and defense. Spirit Empress and Mega Minion provide additional air layers. A more budget-friendly alternative to the LumberLoon freeze package.

archetypes.name: Ram Rider Three Musketeers
archetypes.description: Three Musketeers split-push shell that pairs a Ram Rider cross-lane with a Three Musketeers split on the same turn to create a two-lane attack impossible to defend at equal elixir. Elixir Collector funds the expensive 3M deployment. Ice Spirit and Skeletons cycle cheaply back to the next big pump-and-push window.

archetypes.name: Three Musketeers Lightning
archetypes.description: Three Musketeers beatdown shell that uses Lightning as the primary spell to eliminate defensive buildings and high-HP distraction troops. Dark Prince provides a sturdy low-cost frontline. Bandit and Ice Golem add cross-lane threat. Elixir Collector generates the surplus needed to afford both the 3M and the Lightning in the same sequence.

balance_changes.summary: Reroll Range decreased from 4 tiles to 3 tiles (-25%).
Goblins Spawned on Second Submerge decreased from 2 to 1 (-50%).
First Hit Speed decreased from 1.5s to 1s (-33%).
Reflected Tower Damage decreased from 50 to 38 (-24%).
Collision Radius decreased from 1 tile to 0.75 tiles (-25%).
Death Bomb Damage increased from 209 to 269 (+29%).
Death Bomb Tower Damage decreased from 418 to 269 (-36%).
